title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for command lifecycle, configuration, pipeline, and atomic output and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for import-safe deterministic build pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Atomic Output
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 8274 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Main Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Main Boundary, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Main Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Main Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 1414 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.

For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Main Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A command-line script cannot be both importable and easy to run.

options.text: Declare Static-site publishing and Python reader outcome, content authority, revision, source identity, parser scope, trust policy, route base, accessibility need, and byte, depth, work, and output budgets for the quiz Main Boundary case

Predict Static-site publishing and Python admission, decoding, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the quiz Main Boundary case
Run the smallest deterministic Static-site publishing and Python publication model and retain exact intermediate and changed-case results for the quiz Main Boundary case

Change one Static-site publishing and Python document, delimiter, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, deployment, or recovery condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state production transfer limits for the quiz Main Boundary case

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Atomic Output
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Atomic Output: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 8795 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Atomic Output.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Atomic Output, keep Atomic Output connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Atomic Output, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Deleting output before building is the cleanest safe publication strategy.
